---
title: "Christopher Chizinski"
subtitle: "Human Dimensions of Wildlife & Fisheries"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/images/Chizinski.jpeg
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/chrischizinski
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Lab (UNL)
      href: https://humandimensions.unl.edu
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:cchizinski2@unl.edu
---

[Professor · Assoc. Director of Research & Graduate Education · School of Natural Resources · UNL]{.specimen-label}

I study the **human dimensions of wildlife and fisheries** — the decisions
behind who hunts, fishes, and forages, how those choices change over time, and
what they mean for the agencies charged with managing the resource.

Most of that work runs on survey data and quantitative methods: creel and
angler surveys, capture–recapture, recruitment and retention (R3), and a lot
of reproducible R. Alongside the research, I help lead research and graduate
education in the School of Natural Resources at the University of
Nebraska–Lincoln.

This site collects my [blog](blog.qmd) (mostly R notes), the
[software and dashboards](projects.qmd) my lab builds, and my [CV](cv.qmd).
For lab members, current projects, and openings, see
[humandimensions.unl.edu](https://humandimensions.unl.edu).
